name: Kagoshima Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace and the bright future of the prefecture
the volcanic energy of Sakurajima and the warmth of southern Kyushu's climate

description: A white field charged at the centre with a red prefectural emblem stylising the shape of Kagoshima around Kinko Bay together with the hiragana か.
history: Kagoshima's prefectural symbol was adopted on 14 August 1967; the design abstracts the outline of the Satsuma and Osumi peninsulas around Kinko Bay (with Sakurajima between) into a stylised か, the first hiragana of 'Kagoshima'. The flag places the symbol in red on a white field.

features.role: Kagoshima prefectural emblem (stylised map and hiragana 'ka')
features.symbolism: a stylised emblem combining the outline of Kagoshima Prefecture (including the Satsuma and Osumi peninsulas around Kinko Bay and Sakurajima) with the hiragana か (ka) for 'Kagoshima'
